QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_22

question.text: To be eligible as a sponsor of a mutual fund, the entity must have, among other criteria, at least __________ of experience in financial services with a positive net worth in all preceding 5 years.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI requires a sponsor of a mutual fund to meet four key eligibility criteria: (1) sound track record and general reputation of fairness and integrity, (2) at least 5 years of experience in financial services, (3) positive net worth in all of the immediately preceding 5 years, and (4) the sponsor must be a fit and proper person. The 5-year requirement ensures the sponsor has sufficient experience and financial stability.
**The Trap:** 3 years is too short and does not meet SEBI's requirement. 7 years and 10 years are more stringent than what SEBI mandates. The number 5 applies to both the experience requirement and the net worth assessment period, making it easier to remember.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Sponsor eligibility criteria are tested in NISM V-B. The 5-year experience and 5-year positive net worth requirements are frequently asked.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Sponsor eligibility: 5+ years in financial services, positive net worth in all preceding 5 years, sound track record, fit and proper person.
